title: Safe calls(?.) vs Null checks(!!) in Kotlin
summary: In Kotlin, the type system distinguishes between references that can hold null (nullable references) and those that can not (non-null references). For example, a normal property can’t hold a null value and will show a compile error.
content: In Kotlin, the type system distinguishes between references that can hold null (nullable references) and those that can not (non-null references). For example, a normal property can’t hold a null value and will show a compile error.
